---
title: "{{< var project_title >}}"
subtitle: "{{< var course >}} – Final Project"
root: "."
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
# {{< var project_title >}}

<div class="badge-soft">GBM6330E • Final Project</div>

<p class="lead">
This site is a structured, reproducible project on why and how <strong>diversity</strong> must be included in research that aims to prevent and diagnose Alzheimer’s disease (AD), and how current initiatives and datasets address (or fail to address) this need.
</p>

**How to use this site**
- **Part I** is a guided narrative (definitions, heterogeneity, diversity dimensions, and scope).
- **Part II** is an exploration interface with an **Interactive Explorer** (map + modality diagram), and supporting pages on datasets, atlases/harmonisation, open science, and reproducibility.
- **Original research** links to three reproducible repositories (to be created) corresponding to your project’s research deliverables.

:::

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
### Part I — The need for diversity
Why inclusion matters: what AD is, why it is heterogeneous, and which diversity dimensions impact prevention and diagnosis.

[Open Part I](part1/01-defining-ad.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

::: {.card}
### Part II — Interactive Explorer
Explore datasets and initiatives by **geography** (continent/country) and **data modality** (neuroimaging, genetics/proteomics, exposomics).

[Open Explorer](part2/explorer.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

::: {.card}
### Original research
Three reproducible sub-projects (each intended as its own GitHub repo), linked from the hub site.

[Open Original research](original-research/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

---

## What to edit first

1. Update `data/datasets.json` with your real catalog (as exhaustive as possible).
2. Update `data/region_summaries.json` with your writing + citations.
3. Replace placeholder text in Part I and Part II narrative pages with your analysis.

> Tip: The Interactive Explorer will automatically reflect updates to these JSON files.
